straight_beat.name: Delmar Fontenot
straight_beat.column:
The Astros won yesterday, 7-6, over the Rockies. That’s the tape measure of it. But here’s the thing: you could argue they didn’t need to. The way they won, the way they’ve won lately, feels like a game of whack-a-mole. You hit one problem, and another pops up.  

Take the pitching staff. They’re supposed to be the backbone of this team, right? Well, they’re still figuring out how to be that. The starters are walking a tightrope between

optimist.name: Luz Barquero
optimist.column:
The Astros lost yesterday, but that’s not the story I’m telling. The story is about the hum of the engine, the grind of the tires, and the way a team with a 7-11 record still smells like it’s got gas in the tank. You know what I’m talkin’ about—those 2.5 games behind in the AL West? That’s not a grave. That’s a GPS recalibratin’ before the next leg of the journey.  

Let’s not kid ourselves: the Rockies handed us a wake-up call. Six runs? Yeah, that’s a punch in the gut. But here’s the thing—H-town’s never been about the punches. It’s about the recovery. Bregman’s swing? That’s a pendulum. One at-bat away from flip-floppin’ April into June. You see that? The kid’s got a feel for the game, and the numbers? They’re just the scoreboard’s way of sayin’ “hold on.”

pessimist.name: Coop Yarbrough
pessimist.column:
The Astros lost again. Not just lost, but lost in a way that makes you want to rip your hair out. Colorado’s 6-7 verdict over Houston isn’t just another line in the standings—it’s a mirror held up to a team that’s been stumbling for weeks, choking on its own potential. The record’s 7-11, a number that feels like a countdown to the end of an era. Two and a half games back in the AL West? That’s not a gap; it’s a chasm. And yet, here we are, still pretending this isn’t the same old story.  

Every clean hit they get, I hear a trash can. Can’t unhear it. Nobody can. That’s the punishment and the crime. The Astros keep swinging for the fences, keep trusting their sluggers, and keep getting rewarded with nothing but a few runs and a lot of frustration. They
